Pot 1 (Yellow/Tan).description: Observed in [EARLIEST], [T-5]. Bare soil with dry debris. Not listed in the EXPECTED BIOME REGISTRY. Disappears from frame after [T-5].

Pot 2 (Black).observation: Black pot present throughout (except [T-4]). Initially bare soil with white fragments (eggshells). From [T-3] onwards, contains a small plant with 4-5 rounded, green leaves and an electronic sensor. The plant's morphology does not visually align with typical Mexican Mint.
Pot 2 (Black).reconciliation: The pot matches P2, but the plant is an 'Unidentified Seedling/Plant'. The expected 'Mexican Mint' is either a Systemic Loss or was never identifiable. The electronic sensor and white fragments are 'New Introductions/Interventions'.

Pot 3 (White Cup).description: Observed in [T-1], [CURRENT]. Contains water and a single green leaf/cutting. Not listed in the EXPECTED BIOME REGISTRY.

White Rabbit (5cm) in P3 pot.description: Expected scale anchor and associated pot (P3) are not visible in any image.

Electronic Sensor/Board.description: Introduced into Pot 2 from [T-3] onwards. Not listed in the EXPECTED BIOME REGISTRY.

White Fragments (Eggshells).description: Consistently present in the soil of Pot 2. Not listed in the EXPECTED BIOME REGISTRY, indicating an intentional addition.

P2 (Black Pot).details: The black pot is present and corresponds to P2. However, the plant within it from [T-3] onwards is an unidentified seedling, not visually consistent with 'Mexican Mint'. The original 'Mexican Mint' is considered a Systemic Loss or was never present/identifiable.

White Rabbit (5cm) in P3 pot.details: The expected scale anchor and its associated pot are entirely absent from all images.

biome_observations.Incidental Growth: No visible weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings observed in any pot throughout the sequence.
biome_observations.Debris/Surface Anomalies: Wires are consistently present on the desk surface. A white cylindrical object is visible in [EARLIEST] and [T-5]. A blue object (book/box) and a white pen are visible in [T-3]. White fragments (eggshells) are consistently present in the soil of Pot 2.

temporal_deltas.[EARLIEST] to [T-5]: Minimal to no discernible change. The contents of both visible pots (Yellow/Tan, Black) remain stable.
temporal_deltas.[T-5] to [T-4]: Complete loss of visual information; the image is entirely black.
temporal_deltas.[T-4] to [T-3]: Significant changes: The Yellow/Tan pot (Pot 1) is no longer visible. The Black pot (Pot 2) is now more central and contains a newly emerged, healthy plant with rounded leaves. An electronic sensor with wires has been introduced into Pot 2. A blue object and a white pen appear on the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.[T-3] to [T-2]: The plant in Pot 2 shows slight growth or leaf expansion, maintaining a healthy appearance. Soil moisture appears consistent.
temporal_deltas.[T-2] to [T-1]: A new white cup (Pot 3) containing a plant cutting in water has been introduced. The plant in Pot 2 remains stable in health and size.
temporal_deltas.[T-1] to [CURRENT]: The plant in Pot 2 remains stable. The electronic sensor in Pot 2 now has an illuminated blue LED, indicating active operation. The plant cutting in Pot 3 remains stable.

visual_health_inference.Plant in Pot 2 (Black Pot): Healthy. From its emergence in [T-3] to [CURRENT], the plant consistently displays turgid, uniformly green, rounded leaves. There are no visible signs of wilting, yellowing, browning, or pest damage. Initial growth was observed, followed by a period of stable, robust appearance.
visual_health_inference.Plant in Pot 3 (White Cup): Healthy. From its introduction in [T-1] to [CURRENT], the leaf/cutting in water appears green and intact, showing no signs of decay or wilting.
visual_health_inference.Pot 1 (Yellow/Tan Pot): No plant present for health assessment.

vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a dynamic indoor plant environment over the observed period. A significant systemic loss is the absence of the expected 'White Rabbit in P3 pot'. The black pot (P2) initially contained bare soil but later hosted a healthy, unidentified seedling from [T-3] onwards, which showed initial growth and then maintained stable health. An electronic sensor was also introduced into this pot, becoming active by the final image. A yellow/tan pot (Pot 1), initially present with bare soil, disappeared from the frame after [T-5]. A new white cup (Pot 3) containing a healthy plant cutting was introduced later in the sequence. The overall health of the observed plants is good, with consistent green foliage and no signs of stress. Soil moisture in the black pot was maintained after the plant's emergence. The sequence also included a complete loss of visual data for one period ([T-4]).
